themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are advancing 'national priority' policies, prioritizing citizens over immigrants in public aid, housing, and social services. This theme appears in Spain, Italy, France, and Hungary, with parties like Vox, Lega, Rassemblement National, and Tisza advocating for stricter residency requirements and exclusionary measures.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Proposes 'national priority' in public aid, excluding irregular migrants from social benefits in Andalusia and Madrid.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Supports 'national priority' policies in housing and social services, aligning with Vox in regional coalitions.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Advocates for 'national priority' in welfare and immigration policies, criticizing EU migration policies.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Tisza Party proposes reforms to prioritize Hungarian citizens in social benefits and public services.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Criticizes immigration policies and advocates for prioritizing British citizens in public services.

themes.title: Corruption scandals rock far-right governments
themes.blurb: Corruption allegations are undermining far-right governments and leaders in Argentina, Hungary, and Poland. High-profile figures face investigations for illicit enrichment, misuse of public funds, and nepotism, leading to internal divisions and public backlash.

countries.name: 🇦🇷 La Libertad Avanza
countries.note: Manuel Adorni faces illicit enrichment allegations, sparking internal tensions and public scrutiny.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Péter Magyar's government faces nepotism allegations after nominating his brother-in-law for justice minister.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Former PiS officials and associates face corruption investigations, including child pornography charges.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: EU investigates RN for alleged misuse of EU funds for media training during the 2022 campaign.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Far-right parties are making significant gains in local and regional elections across Europe, challenging traditional parties like Labour and Conservatives. This trend is evident in Germany, Sweden, the UK, and Norway, with parties like AfD, Sverigedemokraterna, and Reform UK leading the surge.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Leads polls in Saxony-Anhalt and gains ground in local elections, surpassing traditional parties.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Gains significant support in polls, nearing 20% and challenging coalition partners.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Wins historic gains in local elections, taking control of councils and unseating Labour in traditional strongholds.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: Gains momentum in local elections, challenging established parties in Norway.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's policies and rhetoric continue to shape right-wing discourse globally, influencing parties in the US, Italy, Poland, and the UK. Key themes include tariffs, immigration, and foreign policy, with Trump's actions often serving as a blueprint for right-wing leaders.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's tariffs, immigration policies, and foreign policy dominate US right-wing discourse.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni aligns with Trump on foreign policy, facing tensions over Iran and trade disputes.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS adopts Trump-like rhetoric on immigration and EU policies, facing internal divisions.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Farage's party mirrors Trump's anti-immigration and anti-establishment rhetoric.

themes.title: EU climate policy sparks cross-national backlash
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are mobilizing against EU climate policies, framing them as economically burdensome and a threat to national sovereignty. This backlash is evident in Poland, Hungary, Germany, and France, with leaders proposing referendums and alternative policies.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: President Nawrocki proposes a referendum on EU climate policies, criticizing their economic impact.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Tisza Party criticizes EU climate policies, advocating for national control over energy and environmental regulations.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD opposes EU climate policies, advocating for energy independence and reduced regulations.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN criticizes EU climate policies, advocating for national sovereignty in environmental decisions.
